## Summary

<!-- What does this PR do and why? -->

## Type of change

- [ ] `feat` — new feature
- [ ] `fix` — bug fix
- [ ] `refactor` — neither fix nor feature
- [ ] `docs` — documentation only
- [ ] `build` / `ci` — tooling or pipeline
- [ ] `chore` — housekeeping

## Breaking change

- [ ] Yes — I have used `!` after the type (e.g. `feat!:`) and/or added a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer
- [ ] No

## Checklist

- [ ] Tests included (feature and fix commits must ship with tests)
- [ ] `dotnet build` passes with zero warnings
- [ ] `dotnet format --verify-no-changes` passes
- [ ] `npx markdownlint-cli2 "**/*.md"` passes (if any `.md` files changed)
- [ ] XML docs updated for any changed public API
